prompt:
Building the backend brain for our team kanban tool. No UI, no storage layer — just the board engine plus a JSON snapshot format, TypeScript on bare node (no deps, tests run with node --test). Two public modules; internals are yours to shape.

board.ts — export class Board.

new Board({ columns, lanes? }) — columns: [{ id, wipLimit? }] in display order, at least one, ids unique; wipLimit when present must be a positive integer (violations are Errors naming the column). lanes: unique strings, defaults to ['default'].

addCard({ id, title, column?, lane? }) — defaults: first column, first lane. Duplicate card id / unknown column / unknown lane -> Error naming the offender. WIP limits bite on add: a column at its limit (cards counted ACROSS lanes) refuses the card with an Error naming the column.

move(id, { column?, lane? }) — either or both; {} is an Error, and a 'move' to the exact spot the card already occupies is an Error saying it's already there. Moving into a column at its WIP limit -> Error naming the column (lane-only shuffles inside a column never hit the limit). Freed space can be used immediately.

find(id) -> { id, title, column, lane } copy. count(columnId). cards(column?, lane?) -> snapshots ordered by column display order then arrival within the column — a card moved out and back re-enters at the tail. Filters validate their column/lane. Unknown ids anywhere -> Error naming the id.

history(id) -> copies of the card's events, oldest first. One global sequence counter across the whole board, starting at 1; every SUCCESSFUL addCard/move consumes the next number (rejected ops consume nothing and record nothing). Shapes: { seq, event: 'created', to: { column, lane } } and { seq, event: 'moved', from: {...}, to: {...} }.

serialize.ts — export serialize(board): string and deserialize(text): Board.

The document is JSON: { columns, lanes, seq, cards } where columns/lanes echo the config, seq is the last consumed sequence number, and cards is an array in cards() order of { id, title, column, lane, history }. serialize must be deterministic (same board -> same string) and round-trip clean: serialize(deserialize(s)) === s. A restored board is fully live — WIP limits enforced, duplicate ids still refused, and the sequence continues from where it stopped (no reused seqs after a restore).

deserialize failures: non-JSON input -> Error mentioning JSON; missing/mis-typed fields -> Error; a card referencing a column or lane the document doesn't declare -> Error naming it.

Acceptance tests: test_board.ts and test_serialize.ts (node --test test_board.ts test_serialize.ts). They're the contract — don't touch them.
